title: Two-way Lingua Libre integration (import open audio + contribute upstream)
context: Lingua Libre is a Wikimedia project hosting open pronunciation recordings. It is a candidate source in sources/allowlist.yml. Per-file CC license verification is required before any Lingua Libre audio is imported, since licenses vary per file and CC-BY-SA share-alike propagates to derivatives. A two-way integration would: (1) import existing open-licensed place-name audio from Lingua Libre into the corpus with per-file license capture and attribution; and (2) enable upstream contribution of new recordings from this corpus back to Lingua Libre. Both directions require the CI allow-list and consent gates to pass. This is a backlog item, unscheduled; size and risk ratings reflect the per-file license verification overhead.
objective: Implement two-way Lingua Libre integration: an import pipeline for existing open-licensed place-name audio (per-file CC license verification, attribution capture, share-alike labelling) and an upstream contribution workflow for exporting corpus recordings to Lingua Libre.
acceptanceCriteria: Import pipeline verifies each Lingua Libre file's license individually before ingesting; CC-BY-SA files are labelled as share-alike in the corpus entry.
Every imported file has a complete Provenance record (source IRI, license, attribution string, capture date) and passes CI gates.
Upstream contribution workflow generates Lingua Libre-compatible metadata for corpus recordings and documents the submission process.
Share-alike propagation for CC-BY-SA imports is correctly labelled per entry and does not silently contaminate CC-BY-4.0 entries.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass.
License reviewer signs off the per-file verification approach before any audio is imported.
Maintainer reviews and merges the PR.
output: Lingua Libre import and contribution pipeline committed via PR with per-file license verification, share-alike labelling, and license reviewer sign-off.
